text:
There once was a quirky old man named Bartholomew who lived at the edge of town. Bartholomew was known far and wide for his penchant for collecting oddities. He had shelves and shelves of books filled with things like turnips, pebbles shaped like cats, and antique spoons. You could find him sitting on his porch reading odd scientific papers about the properties of feathers.

One sunny afternoon, while gazing pensively out at the fluttering butterflies alighting on his garden, Bartholomew spotted something extraordinary. It was a hummingbird bird, but unlike any he'd ever seen. Its violet-and-red feathers resembled the colors of his beloved evening cloak.

Intrigued, Bartholomew hurried to his hut only to find it empty. He whirled his look-through box around frantically, hoping to catch a glimpse of his fabulous new pet. No luck!
